citation: Blockhuys S. et al., Metallomics, 2017, 9, 112-123. DOI: 10.1039/C6MT00202A.
paper_question: What is the human copper proteome, and how is it dysregulated at the transcript level across TCGA cancer types?

gene_category_counts.note: The paper text quotes 12 / ~27 / 15. Our reconstruction from Fig. 1 colors and text yields 12 / 26 / 16. The discrepancy reflects edge cases (CUTC, CUTA, SCO1/2) that the paper itself flags as ambiguous.

data_sources: UniProt.org (search term 'human + copper') with manual curation
Genecards.org (subcellular localization via UniProtKB + COMPARTMENTS confidence 4-5)
TCGA / GDC data portal (upper-quartile normalized FPKM RNA-seq, accessed 22 Aug 2016)
Mertins et al. 2016 (Nature) proteogenomic breast cancer data for Fig. 4

main_breast_cancer_findings.interpretation: Pattern consistent with increased Cu uptake via SLC31A1 and secretory-path delivery via ATOX1 to ATP7B, plus enhanced mitochondrial Cu delivery via COX17 / SCO2.

network_analysis_summary.take_home: The Cu proteome shows high inter-cluster connectivity: many Cu-binding proteins that separate into different heatmap clusters still appear as first-level partners in each other's subnetworks. This motivates a single integrated multi-edge graph for GNN input.

atox1_summary.role: Cytoplasmic Cu chaperone that delivers Cu to ATP7A/B; reported transcription-factor-like activity promoting cyclin D1 and SOD3 expression.
atox1_summary.observation_in_paper: ATOX1 mRNA upregulated in breast, colorectal, uterus and liver tumors (Fig. S1). Protein validated in 67 breast-cancer TMA cores: largely absent in 4 normal cores, present (weak/moderate) in most cancer cores, strongest and most frequent in luminal B.
atox1_summary.subcellular_distribution_in_tissue: Cytoplasmic plus increased nuclear signal in samples with the highest overall ATOX1 levels, consistent with its proposed transcription-factor role.

limitations: RNA does not always reflect protein - only 28/36 Cu-binders had high/moderate mRNA-protein correlation in Fig. 4.
Only 18/25 TCGA cancers had expression data for all 54 genes.
Imbalanced tumor vs. normal sample sizes in TCGA.
IHC cohort small (67 + 4) and unmatched, no significant subtype differences.
The 54-gene list is a lower bound - will grow as more Cu-binders are characterized.
Some gene assignments are inherently ambiguous (SCO1/2 as transporter vs enzyme; CUTC as transporter vs enzyme; CUTA compartment).

potential_relevance_to_lihc.liver_position_in_cluster_tree: group_2 (closest to soft_tissue, kidney, pancreas, bile_duct)
potential_relevance_to_lihc.atox1_direction_in_liver_per_paper_s1: downregulated in liver and bile duct tumors (opposite to breast).
lihc_specific_copper_biology: ATP7B (Wilson's disease) is hepatocyte-specific; Cu export and ceruloplasmin loading happen in the liver.
CP (ceruloplasmin) is liver-secreted; central to plasma Cu distribution.
LOX-family ECM remodelling is relevant to liver fibrosis -> HCC transition.
AFP is a canonical HCC biomarker and is in the 54-gene list (cluster 3, downregulated in breast cluster 3).
potential_relevance_to_lihc.implication_for_our_project: The copper proteome is biologically privileged in liver cancer: we expect that a 54-node graph with LIHC tumor-vs-normal features should carry real signal, especially from ATP7B, CP, ATOX1, AFP and the LOX family.

extraction_confidence.gene_list: medium-high - reconstructed from Fig 1 + text; paper lacks a machine-readable 54-gene table in the main text but Table S1 in ESI confirms it.
extraction_confidence.categories: medium - text and Fig 1 colors agree on most, differ on SCO1/2 and CUTC which are transitional cases flagged by the paper.
extraction_confidence.localizations: medium - taken verbatim from Fig 1 compartment assignments; some proteins are in multiple compartments.
